title: The Second Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## June 22, 2025 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Second Sunday after Pentecost falls on June 22, 2025, placing this sermon in Ordinary Time following the Pentecost season. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would likely include 1 Kings 19:1-15a (Elijah's flight and God's still small voice) and Luke 8:26-39 (the Gerasene demoniac), with Psalm 42 as the appointed psalm. This period represents the long stretch of green Ordinary Time in the Episcopal liturgical calendar, focused on Christian living and discipleship rather than major feast days.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

In June 2025, the Episcopal Church would be in the period following General Convention (held biennially in odd-numbered years), so major legislative actions would have concluded in 2024. The Diocese of Colorado, led by Bishop Kym L. Harris (or her successor if elected in 2024), would be engaged in ordinary diocesan programming. No major Episcopal denominational controversies or bishop elections were anticipated for this specific date, allowing for focus on local parish ministry.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Mid-to-late June in Denver typically brings early summer weather with potential afternoon thunderstorms, a pattern that would likely have affected the region around June 22, 2025. The Denver metro area would be in the thick of summer season with school years ending and outdoor recreation beginning. No specific major breaking news events in the Capitol Hill neighborhood or downtown Denver can be confirmed for this exact week without access to contemporaneous local reporting.

NATIONAL NEWS

The United States in late June 2025 would be approximately six months into President Joe Biden's second term (following the 2024 election cycle). Summer congressional activity would be underway with focus on budgetary matters and any remaining legislative priorities before August recess. The cultural and political temperature would reflect mid-year American life with potential focus on upcoming Independence Day preparations and summer campaign season development.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Global attention in late June 2025 would likely include ongoing developments in Ukraine's conflict with Russia, now in its fourth year since the February 2022 invasion. Middle East tensions, particularly regarding Gaza and regional stability following 2024 developments, would remain in international news cycles. Any specific crisis or diplomatic breakthrough for this exact week cannot be specified without contemporaneous sources.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in late June 2025 would be in summer entertainment season with major film releases and the beginning of summer concert tours and festivals. The country would be transitioning into the Independence Day period with patriotic themes emerging in public spaces and discourse. A general sense of mid-year routine would characterize American life—neither the intensity of election season nor the holiday season, but rather the practical rhythms of summer living and family planning.
